articles.title: Prime Day 2026: Best Home Cinema Deals
articles.description: Discover top savings on OLED TVs, soundbars, and projectors during Prime Day. Our experts provide real-time recommendations for the best deals available.
articles.impact: Prime Day offers significant savings on home cinema.

articles.title: Samsung Confirms Galaxy S27 Pro Launch
articles.description: Samsung officially acknowledges the Galaxy S27 Pro, part of a new series expected next year. This includes successors to the Galaxy S26 lineup and a new model.
articles.impact: Samsung's new series boosts its flagship offerings.

articles.title: Porsche Reveals 911 GT4 R as Cayman Successor
articles.description: Porsche introduces the 911 GT4 R, set to replace the 718 Cayman GT4 RS Clubsport in GT4 racing next year. This model is based on the 911 Cup.
articles.impact: Porsche strengthens its GT4 racing lineup.

articles.title: Google Play Store Opens to External Billing
articles.description: Starting June 30, Google will allow external payment options in the Play Store for users in the US, UK, and Europe. This marks a significant policy change.
articles.impact: Google's change enhances payment flexibility for users.

articles.title: iOS 27 Home App Introduces New Features
articles.description: Apple's iOS 27 update enhances the Home app with over ten new HomeKit features, improving functionality for Secure Video cameras and more.
articles.impact: iOS 27 boosts smart home capabilities significantly.

articles.title: New Site Shames Companies Lacking Passkeys
articles.description: A new website highlights that 24% of popular sites still don't support passkeys, the most secure login method. This raises concerns about online security.
articles.impact: Increased awareness of security shortcomings online.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Launch Confirmed
articles.description: Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra and other devices are confirmed for a July Unpacked event, clearing the way for their U.S. launch.
articles.impact: Samsung's foldables set to make a strong debut.

articles.title: Nothing Launches Mini Phone Under $320
articles.description: Nothing is releasing a mini phone, reviving the concept Apple abandoned in 2021. Priced under $320, it aims for compact usability.
articles.impact: Nothing's mini phone targets niche market demand.
